channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Home Depot's Presidents' Day sale is live – up to 50% off appliances, furniture and tools
item.description: The 2025 Home Depot Presidents' Day sale is live, and I'm rounding up all the best deals on appliances, tools, furniture, and more.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Never lose cell service again with this space-age satellite tech
item.description: AST SpaceMobile of Texas is developing the world's first global cellular broadband network that can connect directly to your smartphone without special equipment.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Exponential growth but for how long: DeepSeek's meteoric rise in AI-land continues unabated, fresh Similarweb data shows
item.description: Data from Similarweb shows DeepSeek is now the third most used AI tool, but is its popularity already waning?
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: Sony PlayStation Network outage enrages gamers around the world
item.description: Sony’s PlayStation Network (PSN) is experiencing a major outage across the world, leaving tens of thousands of gamers unable to access online services, stores and multimedia apps.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Amazon to pay nearly $4M to settle lawsuit alleging it took tips from drivers
item.description: Amazon has agreed to pay nearly $4 million to settle charges that the e-commerce company subsidized its labor costs by taking tips its delivery drivers received from customers, District of Columbia Attorney General Brian L. Schwalb said Friday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Severance director Ben Stiller says work is underway on season 3's scripts – and he hopes it won't take three years to make
item.description: Apple hasn't renewed Severance for a third season yet, but its creative team is already hard at work on developing it.
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: SVG files are offering cybercriminals an easy way in with new phishing attacks
item.description: Scalable vector graphics can render hyperlinks in browsers, drawing victims closer to getting their credentials stolen.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: AWS plans to outspend Microsoft and Google on on AI infrastructure
item.description: Amazon Web Services (AWS) intends to invest $100 billion in ramping up infrastructure for AI cloud services this year, surpassing the spending plans of rivals Microsoft and Google.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: OpenAI looks across US for sites to build its Trump-backed Stargate AI data centers
item.description: OpenAI is scouring the U.S. for sites to build a network of huge data centers to power its artificial intelligence technology, expanding beyond a flagship Texas location and looking across 16 states to accelerate the Stargate project championed by President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Oracle Fights to Keep JavaScript Trademark Amid External Pressure
item.description: Oracle files motion for dismissal of part of a petition to cancel the JavaScript trademark amid external pressure from Deno and Node.js creator Ryan Dahl.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Fortinet targets branch offices with upgraded firewalls
item.description: Fortinet has refreshed its branch office firewalls, adding power and features to its next-generation FortiGate G-series devices.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Airlines are finally embracing Apple’s AirTags—which means lost luggage could be a thing of the past
item.description: There’s nothing more annoying than arriving at your destination and finding that your checked baggage didn’t make the trip. But thanks to Apple’s new partnership with 15 different airlines, it’s easier than ever to track down your lost luggage—provided you have the right $29 gadget.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: Lawmakers warn hiring freeze could thwart cyber workforce efforts
item.description: A former White House official who led cyber and tech recruiting efforts said the hiring freeze "will set us back months if not years." The post Lawmakers warn hiring freeze could thwart cyber workforce efforts first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Google Updates Gemini Portfolio Amid Rising Demand for Low-Cost AI Models
item.description: Google updates its Gemini portfolio, releasing a budget-friendly model amid growing demand for low-cost AI models driven by the rise of Chinese competitor DeepSeek.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: One Tech Tip: How to block your phone from tracking your location
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Smartphones are useful tools for everyday life, but they're privy to nearly everything about you, including all the places you've been — if you let them.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Legislators demand truth about OPM email server
item.description: Two members of the US House of Representatives want answers from the US Office of Personnel Management (OPM) over allegations that a “server of unknown nature” was used last month to “access sensitive government data without regard for crucial security and privacy protections.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Google scraps its diversity hiring goals as it complies with Trump's new government contractor rules
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google is scrapping some of its diversity hiring targets, joining a lengthening list of U.S. companies that have abandoned or scaled back their diversity, equity and inclusion programs.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: DOGE’s ‘unimpeded’ access to classified data poses national, economic security risks
item.description: There is no information on who has been hired under DOGE, the authority under which it operates, or how it vets and monitors its staff. The post DOGE’s ‘unimpeded’ access to classified data poses national, economic security risks first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Obis, Federal News Network

item.title: Fast fashion, laptops and toys are likely to cost more due to US tariffs on Chinese imports
item.description: A sweeping new U.S. tariff on products made in China is expected to increase the prices American consumers pay for a wide array of products, from the ultra-cheap apparel sold on online shopping platforms to toys and electronic devices such as computers and cellphones.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Hands-on with the Windows answer to Apple’s Vision Pro
item.description: Hey, remember the Apple Vision Pro? For a while there, everyone was talking about Apple’s $3,499 headset being the future of productivity. You’d strap a headset to your face and work on large monitors floating in a “mixed reality” world.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How to bring Google’s custom vibration brilliance to any Android phone today
item.description: Have you heard the buzz? Good vibrations are on their way to Android — or at least to Google’s own homemade Pixel devices.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Apple lashes out at iPhone porn app maker and the EU rules allowing its download
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Apple has chided a newly introduced pornography app available in the European Union, warning that the bloc’s digital rules opening the way for third-party app store downloads undermines consumer confidence in the tech giant.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Smart Traffic Enforcement: Kazakhstan’s Qorgau System in Action
item.description: Traffic management is a growing challenge for cities worldwide, requiring a balance between enforcement, efficiency, and public trust. In Kazakhstan, the Qorgau system is redefining road safety through an innovative fusion of artificial intelligence (AI), computer vision, and mobile technology.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Judge says Elon Musk's claims of harm from OpenAI are a 'stretch' but welcomes possible trial
item.description: OAKLAND, Calif. (AP) — Elon Musk's lawyers faced off with OpenAI in court Tuesday as a federal judge weighed the billionaire's request for a court order that would block the ChatGPT maker from converting itself to a for-profit company. U.S.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY and MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Federal Executive Forum IT Modernization and Transformation Strategies in Government Progress and Best Practices 2025
item.description: How are agencies profiling a successful IT modernization strategy and what is the vision for the future? The post Federal Executive Forum IT Modernization and Transformation Strategies in Government Progress and Best Practices 2025 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Rising crypto popularity sparks surge in scams: Avoid falling prey
item.description: As cryptocurrency gains popularity, it also becomes a prime target for scammers, making it crucial to take precautions and avoid falling prey to scams.
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Joyce, NewsNation

item.title: Anthropic unveils new framework to block harmful content from AI models
item.description: Anthropic has showcased a new security framework, designed to reduce the risk of harmful content generated by its large language models (LLM), a move that could have far-reaching implications for enterprise tech companies.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: VMware by Broadcom: Product, service and support news
item.description: News of Broadcom’s plans to acquire VMware triggered concerns among customers, partners, and industry watchers, many of whom recalled Broadcom’s track record with prior high-profile acquisitions, including network switching vendor Brocade in 2016 for almost $6 billion, software firm CA Technologies
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Microsoft founder Bill Gates explores the making of his internal operating system in new memoir
item.description: As he prepares to turn 70 later this year, Microsoft founder Bill Gates' new memoir explores how his childhood quirks, upbringing, friendships and experiences coalesced into shaping his internal operating system.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Most talked-about Super Bowl ads
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says Super Bowl 2025 ads promise star power and laughs, featuring Beckham's twin reveal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bill Gates shares his thoughts on vaccine backlash, Intel's woes and Google's antitrust battle
item.description: Although Microsoft founder Bill Gates sat down for an interview with The Associated Press to discuss his new memoir, “Source Code: My Beginnings,” he also shared his views on a variety of other topics, including vaccine conspiracy views about him, his thoughts on the struggles of longtime computer c
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: SoftBank and OpenAI set up joint company to push artificial intelligence services
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japanese technology giant SoftBank Group and OpenAI stepped up their AI partnership Monday, setting up a 50-50 held company called SB OpenAI Japan.
item.dc:creator: YURI KAGEYAMA, Associated Press, Fox 5
